title: Adult Day Programs - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Adult Day Programs 27 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness ALBERTINA KERR INCLUSIVE DAY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides recreational, social, and therapeutic services both on-site and in the community. Individuals can participate in arts and crafts, exercise programs, outdoor excursions, music exploration, and other group activities. The service is geared towards individuals wanting a day program who do not have employment prioritized. 424 NE 22nd Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Ages 18 and older with a documented disability Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (503) 239-8101 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ARC LANE COUNTY STEPPING OUT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support to adults who experience intellectual and/or developmental disabilities to learn new skills, explore and recreate within their communities, strengthen interpersonal skills, and make new friends. 4181 E Street Springfield, OR 97478 Eligibility: Age 18 and older with intellectual and/or developmental disabilities Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 852-5785 142 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY SOCIAL SERVICES OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE–MEDICAID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides in-home care. Services include housekeeping, personal care, laundry, shopping, transportation, and meal preparation and delivery. Adult day services (non-residential community-based programs, providing services for part of the day). Registered nurse services (one-time or intermittent assessment, service planning, provider training, and education). Also offers case management and options counseling (counseling to support informed decision-making on services based on strengths, needs, and preferences). Service hours per person vary depending on assessed need and funding amounts. In-home service hours are available up to 20 hours per week, based on individual need. Benefits will not be subject to estate recovery. 2051 Kaen Road #135 Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Adults, age 18 and older, who meet the following criteria: -Need help from another person with personal care tasks -Income no greater than 400% of the Federal Poverty Level -Assets no greater than the average cost of six months in a nursing facility -Currently living in their own home and not a provider-operator or owned facility or foster/care home Hours: Monday-Thursday 7am-6pm (503) 655-8640 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COMMUNITY ACTION PROGRAM OF EAST CENTRAL OREGON HERMISTON OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE – MEDICAID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides in-home care. Services include housekeeping, personal care, laundry, shopping, transportation, and meal preparation and delivery. Also offers case management and options counseling (counseling to support informed decision-making on services based on strengths, needs, and preferences).
